rationale: A is better anchored in Flink runtime classes, names concrete call sites and neighboring tests, and enumerates the full metric set with an explicit uncertainty note. B is too vague for an engineer and appears to speculate about which fields are reporting-relevant, while also missing specific test anchors and overfocusing on a subset of metrics.

A: Some citation anchors look synthetic or unverified, especially the `#part-0001` doc fragments.
It states a field-to-caller relationship without proving which reporting code reads each getter.
B: Omits several concrete metric fields and exact neighboring tests, reducing usefulness for code changes.
Introduces uncertainty around `bytesBufferedInAlignment`, which may not be a real 2.2.0 API field.
